creator: _jkrow
caption: Did you know you can actually swim in the Amazon River?! 🫢 🎥 @_jkrow, T+L contributor. #traveltips #ecudaor #amazonrainforest

transcription.text: i spent four days in the ecuadorian amazon and as a seasoned adventure traveler this is what shocked me the most. first surprise was how hard it was to actually get there. our trip started with a 10-hour overnight bus from quito, then another bus, and then finally a three-hour canoe ride down the amazon river to reach our lodge. by the time we got there we were exhausted, very sweaty, and very aware that there was no quick way back out. next was the fact that the experience was a lot about what we heard. the rainforest is loud with birds calling from every direction all day long with songs that i had never heard before. yes we saw a lot of animals but they blend in really well and you have to slow way down and retrain your eyes if you want to spot them. also did you know that you can swim in the amazon river? i didn't but i also wasn't brave enough to because yes there are piranhas in there. but almost every night ended on the water, drifting through a lagoon at sunset, and taking an evening swim. finally there were the mosquitoes. maybe it was just a season but they ended up being way less of an issue than i had made up in my head. despite all of this there definitely were things that we did expect like these giant spiders, huge cockroaches, and ants that could really ruin your day. which weren't unbearable especially if you're not really scared of bugs. they're just a reminder that you're deep in the amazon rainforest.
